elements.title: Strand Arcade
elements.contents: ... that Strand Arcade (pictured) was the "most important commercial centre in New Zealand in the early 1900s and beyond", according to Heritage New Zealand?

elements.title: David E. Mitchell
elements.contents: ... that, after David E. Mitchell was diagnosed with cancer that cost $440,000 annually to treat, he founded Patients for Affordable Drugs Now to challenge drug companies that act like "Tony Soprano"?

elements.title: Telangana Gaddar Film Awards
elements.contents: ... that the Telangana Gaddar Film Awards are named after a person who rejected awards for Telugu cinema?

elements.title: Venda
elements.contents: ... that the association of Venda kings with mountains and subordinate rulers with pools affected the layout of a settlement?

elements.title: Vidoe Smilevski
elements.contents: ... that Vidoe Smilevski's apartment was "a center of illegal revolutionary activity" before he became the Macedonian president?

elements.title: Arena
elements.contents: ... that Arena is the first novel to be set in the universe of the card game Magic: The Gathering?

elements.title: Fiji beat Australia 1–0 in 1988
elements.contents: ... that, before Fiji beat Australia 1–0 in 1988, the Fiji Football Association offered each player a FJ$500 bonus if they won?

elements.title: OpenDesk
elements.contents: ... that the International Criminal Court decided to switch from Microsoft Office to OpenDesk after its chief prosecutor was disconnected from his Microsoft-hosted email account?

elements.title: Guillermo Pfening
elements.contents: ... that Argentine actor Guillermo Pfening and a female friend agreed to have a child together if they were not parents by the age of 35?
